topic: Four Pillars of OOP in C++
description: Comprehensive coverage of Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction. Each concept explained patiently with many code examples.

sections.title: Encapsulation
sections.definition: Bundling data and methods together, hiding internal state using private/protected access specifiers. Exposes only necessary public interface.

sections.title: Inheritance – Basics
sections.definition: Derived class inherits members from base class. Promotes code reuse. Syntax: class Derived : access-specifier Base

sections.title: Types of Inheritance
sections.definition: Single, Multiple, Multilevel, Hierarchical, Hybrid. Each has different structures.

sections.title: Access Specifiers in Inheritance
sections.definition: public inheritance: public stays public, protected stays protected. protected: public becomes protected. private: all become private.

sections.title: Constructor and Destructor in Inheritance
sections.definition: Base constructor called first, then derived. Destructor order: derived first, then base.
sections.output:
Base constructor
Derived constructor
Derived destructor
Base destructor

sections.title: Polymorphism – Compile-time (Function Overloading)
sections.definition: Polymorphism means 'many forms'. Compile-time polymorphism includes function overloading and operator overloading.

sections.title: Polymorphism – Runtime (Virtual Functions)
sections.definition: Runtime polymorphism uses virtual functions. Base class pointer/reference can call derived class overrides. Requires virtual keyword.

sections.title: Abstraction
sections.definition: Hiding implementation details and showing only essential features. Achieved via abstract classes (with pure virtual functions) or interfaces.
